creator: kris@FreeBSD.org
text:
Andrey Lykhin wrote:

> atapci0: <ServerWorks HT1000 SATA150 controller> port 0x2040-0x2047,0x2034-0x2037,0x2038-0x203f,0x2030-0x2033,0x2000-0x201f mem 0xdf900000-0xdf901fff irq 7 at device 14.0 on pci1
> ata2: <ATA channel 0> on atapci0
> ata3: <ATA channel 1> on atapci0
> ata4: <ATA channel 2> on atapci0
> ata5: <ATA channel 3> on atapci0
> atapci1: <ServerWorks HT1000 UDMA100 controller> port 0x1f0-0x1f7,0x3f6,0x170-0x177,0x376,0x1c00-0x1c0f at device 2.1 on pci0
> ata0: <ATA channel 0> on atapci1
> ata1: <ATA channel 1> on atapci1
> ad4: 476940MB <SAMSUNG HD501LJ CR100-12> at ata2-master SATA150
> ad6: 476940MB <SAMSUNG HD501LJ CR100-12> at ata3-master SATA150
> Trying to mount root from ufs:/dev/ad4s1a
> bge0: link state changed to UP
> 
>> How-To-Repeat:
> Install FreeBSD 6.3-RELEASE (or FreeBSD 6.2-RELEASE or FreeBSD 7.0-RC1) on HP ProLiant DL145G3 with HDD SAMSUNG HD501LJ.

It is unlikely to be your disks, rather your ATA controller.  I believe 
there are known issues with ServerWorks HT1000 controllers.

Kris
